div.h2: PTRACE_ATTACH Sequence — Attaching to a Running Process

g.text: Step 2 — Kernel Delivers SIGSTOP to Target
Step 3 — Set Options and Begin Tracing (CORRECT PATH)
struct user_regs_struct (at EXIT stop after mid-syscall attach)
orig_rax | long | 0 = SYS_read (preserved — use to name syscall)
rdi | ullong | 0 (arg1: fd — may be stale)
rsi | ullong | 0x7fff... (arg2: buf ptr — stale, old addrspace)
rdx | ullong | 1024 (arg3: count — may be stale)
rip | ullong | kernel ret addr (not meaningful to tracer)
Rule: at EXIT stop, ONLY rax and orig_rax are reliable. Args may be clobbered.

text.tspan: Target runs until next syscall boundary
RULE: PTRACE_ATTACH is ASYNC. The target is NOT stopped when the call returns.
Any ptrace operation before waitpid() succeeds will return ESRCH or EIO.

g.text: Target was INSIDE a blocking syscall (e.g. read() waiting on stdin) when PTRACE_ATTACH fired
Expected (normal start) | ENTRY → EXIT → ENTRY → EXIT
Fix | detect EXIT with in_syscall==0 → skip print

text.tspan: next stop will be normal ENTRY
